segments.text: When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John
Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples
He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee
And he must needs go through Samaria
Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph
Now Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour
There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water:
Jesus saith unto her
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat
How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus answered and said unto her
Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus answered and said unto her
Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus saith unto her
I have no husband
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Thou hast well said, I have no husband
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus saith unto her
I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: Jesus saith unto her
And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men
Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
segments.speaker: Samaritan Woman

segments.text: In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying
Master, eat
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: But he said unto them
Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Jesus saith unto them
And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did
Then said Jesus unto him
Sir, come down ere my child die
segments.speaker: Nobleman

segments.text: Go thy way; thy son liveth
segments.speaker: Jesus
